hypothesis.original_question: A 3D-printed hydroxyapatite scaffold with a gradient pore size (from 100 μm to 500 μm) will exhibit higher osteoblast proliferation rates compared to a uniform pore structure because it better mimics the natural transition between cortical and cancellous bone.

refined.intervention: A 3D-printed phase-pure hydroxyapatite scaffold (cylinder, 6 mm Ø × 4 mm tall) with a continuous radial pore-size gradient from 100 μm at the outer surface to 500 μm at the central axis, fabricated by DLP vat-photopolymerization of an HA-loaded photocurable slurry, debinded and sintered to a phase-pure HA scaffold.
refined.outcome: Osteoblast (MC3T3-E1 Subclone 4, ATCC CRL-2593) proliferation rate quantified by total dsDNA content (PicoGreen) and orthogonally by metabolic activity (alamarBlue), with DAPI confocal cross-sections to verify spatial distribution.
refined.threshold: Day-14 dsDNA content per scaffold ≥ 25% higher (one-sided two-sample t-test, α = 0.05, n = 6 scaffolds per group per time point) on the gradient-pore scaffold than on a uniform 300 μm pore-size HA scaffold matched on total porosity (±3 pp), mass (±5%), surface area (BET, ±10%), HA phase purity (XRD, β-TCP fraction < 5%), and Ca/P ratio (ICP-MS, 1.667 ± 0.05).
refined.mechanism: A continuous 100→500 μm radial gradient (a) reproduces the cortical→cancellous architecture, (b) provides a small-pore outer shell for early attachment AND a large-pore core for nutrient/waste diffusion, (c) avoids the diffusion-limited necrotic core seen in uniform large-pore scaffolds and the cellular-skin / nutrient-bridge effect seen in uniform small-pore scaffolds, and (d) introduces graded curvature/permeability cues consistent with reported pore-curvature → osteogenesis findings (Yang 2022 TPMS β-TCP).
refined.control: Uniform 300 μm pore HA scaffold of equivalent outer geometry, total porosity, mass, surface area (BET), HA chemistry (XRD-verified phase-pure HA, ICP-MS Ca/P), and outer surface area, fabricated by the same DLP printer with the same slurry batch, debinding, and sintering schedule, seeded and cultured identically in parallel on the same plate / bioreactor.

novelty.rationale: Two close-but-imperfect precedents exist. Daskalakis 2024 (filename slug 2024-diez-escudero) fabricated extrusion-printed PCL/HA composite bone bricks with a three-zone radial pore-size gradient and reported a monotonic increase in cell number from inner (small-pore) to outer (large-pore) regions in hADSC d14 MTT — supporting a gradient effect, but in PCL/HA composite at 52% porosity, not pure-HA, and the gradient was three discrete zones rather than continuous. Conoscenti 2025 produced a continuous 70→200 μm pore-size gradient in PLLA via thermally-induced phase separation and showed pore size alone biases hMSC fate under identical media — the strongest direct mechanistic evidence for a continuous gradient effect, but in PLLA (osteochondral scaffold), not HA, and at a smaller pore range (70–200 μm) below the proposed 100–500 μm window. Wang 2024 extrusion-printed a CDHA gradient-pore scaffold (closest pure-CaP precedent) but the gradient parameters were not reported as 100–500 μm and the primary readouts were tumor and BMSC/HUVEC, not parametric osteoblast proliferation. No paper fabricates a continuous radial pure-HA pore gradient at 100→500 μm with osteoblast-proliferation as the primary endpoint. Hypothesis is plausibly novel.

references.title: In Vitro Evaluation of Pore Size Graded Bone Scaffolds with Different Material Composition
references.authors: Daskalakis, Huang, Hassan, Omar, Vyas, Acar, Fallah, Cooper, Weightman, Blunn, Koc, Bartolo
references.relevance: Closest direct precedent: 3D-printed three-zone radial pore gradient PCL/HA bone bricks; PCL/HA wins d14 proliferation (243,413 ± 22,484 cells vs PCL 186,579 ± 14,221, p = 0.0003, +30%), and cell number monotonically increases inner→outer pore region.

references.title: Continuous pore size gradient enhances zonal-specific differentiation of stem cells in an osteochondral scaffold
references.authors: Conoscenti, Smith, Pirosa, Pavia, Zhang, La Carrubba, Brucato, Tuan, Gottardi
references.relevance: Strongest mechanistic precedent for continuous pore gradient driving zonal cell response — pore size alone biases fate under identical media (PLLA TIPS, 70→200 μm).

references.title: Polydopamine-functionalized calcium-deficient hydroxyapatite 3D-printed scaffold ...
references.relevance: Closest pure-CaP gradient-pore precedent: extrusion-printed CDHA scaffold with explicit gradient pore structure supports BMSC proliferation and ALP/RUNX2/OPN expression.

literature_cautions.issue: Pore-size optimum is genuinely contested across the literature (200–350 μm vs 300–500 μm vs 600 μm vs hierarchical).

Native rabbit-femur Haversian/Volkmann canal diameters are 40–200 μm — the 500 μm core of the proposed gradient is closer to trabecular spacing than cortical canal diameter; mechanism statement should reflect this.
Karageorgiou & Kaplan 2005 (canonical '100 μm minimum, ~300 μm optimal') is non-OA and not in raw/; cited transitively via Loh 2013, Polo-Corrales 2014, Baino 2015, Bruzauskaite 2016.

At least one HA-scaffold + osteogenesis claim (Wang 2020 grooved-HA / mitochondria) was retracted in 2025 for image-reuse; treat HA-osteogenesis claims with caution and cross-validate every imaging-based readout.

protocol.title: Generate parametric CAD: gradient and uniform-pore scaffolds

failure_warnings.mitigation: Treat the CAD pore-size axis as a target, not the truth. Build a parametric model that allows S = d_post/d_CAD compensation per pore-bin from Step 2's calibration; do not lock final scaffold geometry until Step 2 produces a measured shrinkage-factor lookup. TPMS / gyroid-style implicit-surface gradient is preferred (better CAD-to-physical fidelity) over an orthogonal lattice.
Use a continuous radial pore-size field (voxel-by-voxel), not a discrete-zone/biphasic design — continuous gradients avoid stress concentrators at zone boundaries (Liu 2025, Xie 2025).

protocol.rationale: The hypothesis specifies a continuous 100→500 μm radial gradient. Two cylindrical 6 mm Ø × 4 mm scaffolds are designed: (a) gradient — pore-size field d(r) = 100 μm at r = 3 mm (outer surface) increasing linearly to 500 μm at r = 0 (central axis); (b) uniform — pore size 300 μm throughout. Both are matched on total porosity (target 65 ± 3 pp by integration over the gradient profile), outer geometry, and mass. The 6 × 4 mm geometry caps the smallest dimension at 4 mm to limit but not eliminate the diffusion-limited-core failure mode (a 2D core thickness tests the gradient's diffusion-mitigation claim). TPMS gyroid topology is selected because of its well-characterised CAD-to-physical relationship.

protocol.title: Print and sinter calibration array; build CAD-to-measured shrinkage lookup

reagents.notes: Sintering-validated medical-grade HA powder; Sigma 21223 acceptable as alternate.

failure_warnings.mitigation: Print and sinter a 6×6 array with CAD pores 50/100/150/200/300/500/700 μm; μCT post-sinter; compute S = d_post/d_CAD per pore bin; build a lookup so the experimental CAD is pre-compensated (e.g., 100 μm target → ~125 μm CAD if S ≈ 0.80).
Cap sintering at 1200–1250 °C; verify Ca/P ratio of starting powder by ICP-MS = 1.667 ± 0.05; XRD post-sinter to confirm β-TCP fraction < 5%.
Slow debinding ramp 0.5 °C/min through 300–600 °C with ≥ 2 h soak; post-sinter EtOH/water reflux leach; ISO-10993-5 cytotoxicity screen on L929 fibroblasts before MC3T3 seeding.
Pair μCT (≤10 μm voxel, full-volume) with SEM cross-sections at 50–500× to verify thresholding; mercury intrusion porosimetry on a sister scaffold for an independent pore-throat distribution.

protocol.rationale: Every claim of '100→500 μm gradient' fails without μCT verification: AM resolution + slurry rheology + sintering shrinkage compound to shift pores 15–25% and obliterate features below ~80 μm. Ishack 2015 robocasted HA/β-TCP at 1100 °C × 4 h. Yang 2022 used DLP-of-β-TCP with TPO photoinitiator. Bose 2017 cites 60–70 wt% HA loading minimises shrinkage. The output is a per-batch S = d_post/d_CAD lookup table that defines the actual experimental CAD geometry at Step 3.

protocol.title: Fabricate experimental scaffolds (gradient and uniform)

failure_warnings.mitigation: Print half of each batch with the cylinder axis vertical, half horizontal — Yang 2022 standard practice — and test for orientation effects on pore distribution and downstream cell response.
Use a single HA powder lot for the entire study; ICP-MS Ca/P at lot receipt; XRD on every sintered batch.
Compression test 3 sister scaffolds per group destructively; reject the batch if Young's modulus drops >2× from the design FE prediction (indicates microcracks).

protocol.rationale: Print n = 36 gradient + n = 36 uniform scaffolds (60 for the 5×6 time-point experiment + 12 spares for QC, characterization, and replacement). Use the Step-2 shrinkage lookup to pre-compensate CAD pore sizes. Same slurry, same debinding, same sintering run for both groups so post-sinter chemistry / surface state is matched.

protocol.title: Characterise each batch: μCT, XRD, BET, ICP-MS, mechanical

failure_warnings.mitigation: μCT every experimental scaffold post-sinter at ≤ 10 μm voxel; threshold and compute pore-size histogram per radial bin. Reject any scaffold whose measured outer-zone pore mean falls outside 100 ± 25 μm or core mean outside 500 ± 50 μm.
XRD on one scaffold per batch (Cu-Kα, 2θ = 20–60°, 0.02° step, 2 s dwell); Rietveld-quantify β-TCP/(HA + β-TCP). Reject batch if β-TCP fraction > 5%.
ICP-MS Ca/P on acid-digested scaffold (one per batch); confirm 1.667 ± 0.05.

protocol.rationale: Match-on-confound parameters must be verified, not assumed: total porosity (gas pycnometer or μCT thresholding), surface area (BET N₂ adsorption), HA phase purity (XRD), Ca/P stoichiometry (ICP-MS). Mercury intrusion porosimetry on a sister scaffold cross-checks the μCT pore-size distribution. Mechanical compression test (3 sister scaffolds per group, destructive) confirms the gradient produces graded modulus. Acceptance criteria: gradient and uniform groups within ±3 pp porosity, ±10% BET, β-TCP < 5% by XRD, Ca/P 1.667 ± 0.05.

protocol.title: Sterilise scaffolds; ISO-10993-5 cytotoxicity pre-screen on L929

failure_warnings.mitigation: Soxhlet-style EtOH/water reflux leach for 24 h; vacuum dry; gamma-sterilise (25 kGy) or autoclave (121 °C × 30 min, dry cycle). Run ISO-10993-5 cytotoxicity on L929 fibroblasts (24 h scaffold-conditioned medium, n = 6) — viability ≥ 80% required to release the batch for MC3T3 work.

protocol.rationale: Residual TPO is the dominant cytotoxicity risk for DLP HA scaffolds and is responsible for confounding 'gradient improves proliferation' claims when the uniform control happens to have residual photoinitiator. ISO-10993-5 is the standard pre-screen and Thammarakcharoen 2026 used this exact pipeline for binder-jet HA.

protocol.title: Receive cells, expand, STR-authenticate, mycoplasma-screen

reagents.notes: Ascorbic-acid-free formulation for parental MC3T3 maintenance.
Lock a single FBS lot for the entire study.

failure_warnings.mitigation: Source MC3T3-E1 Subclone 4 explicitly from ATCC CRL-2593 (CVCL_5440) — high-mineraliser, the canonical bone-TE subclone. Do NOT use the parental line (CVCL_0409) or Subclone 14 (CVCL_5437, ATCC CRL-2594), and do NOT trust the wiki source file `cellosaurus-mc3t3-e1-subclone4-cvcl5437.json` whose CVCL ID is mis-labelled (it is Subclone 14, not 4) — Pass 2 flagged this.
STR-authenticate at receipt (mouse 9-locus panel; PubMed 37712227 reference profile); reject if any locus mismatches the ATCC reference.

protocol.rationale: The wiki carries an explicit data-integrity flag: the catalog file labelled 'subclone 4' references CVCL_5437, which is actually Subclone 14. The hypothesis specifies osteoblasts (not osteo-precursors with subclone-dependent variability), so Subclone 4 (CRL-2593) is the canonical choice and STR authentication at receipt is non-negotiable. Use < passage 10 throughout the study to control for proliferation-rate drift.

protocol.title: Pre-coat scaffolds, dynamic-perfusion seed at 5 × 10⁴ MC3T3 per scaffold

failure_warnings.mitigation: Static seeding loses 30–60% of cells through 500 μm pores. Use dynamic perfusion seeding (oscillating flow, 0.5 mL/min for 4 h, scaffold in custom holder) — Conoscenti 2025 reported 22.8 ± 1.3% efficiency uniformly across a gradient. Pre-coat scaffolds with 10 μg/mL fibronectin × 1 h at 37 °C to improve initial adhesion to large struts.

Dynamic perfusion is the dominant mitigation. As secondary check, image one sentinel scaffold per group at 24 h with DAPI cross-sections to verify radial cell distribution before counting day-1 endpoints.

Recover medium from the perfusion outlet at 4 h and count by hemocytometer; report seeding efficiency per scaffold as primary QC. Reject any scaffold where seeded count differs from group mean by > 2 SD.

protocol.rationale: Static seeding is a confound for any large-pore scaffold (>400 μm) and biases the readout in favour of small-pore scaffolds, which would falsely inflate the gradient effect (since the small-pore outer shell of the gradient seeds more efficiently than the uniform 300 μm control). Conoscenti 2025 demonstrated that dynamic perfusion gives uniform seeding across a 70→200 μm gradient. 5 × 10⁴ cells per scaffold matches the hypothesis specification.

protocol.title: Culture in proliferation medium (no osteogenic induction) with daily medium change

failure_warnings.mitigation: The 4 mm scaffold height is at the static-culture diffusion-limit threshold. Use perfusion bioreactor (0.5 mL/min continuous, ≥ 1× scaffold-volume turnover per minute) for ALL groups. Predict permeability a priori via CFD (target ≥ 10⁻⁹ m²); if uniform-pore control falls below threshold the experiment will look gradient-favouring purely due to diffusion confound.

Run the primary experiment in proliferation medium (NO β-glycerophosphate, NO ascorbic-acid-2-phosphate, NO dexamethasone) so cells stay in proliferative phase through d14. Differentiation markers are tracked separately (Step 11) on a parallel cohort with osteogenic medium.

Pre-register d14 as the primary endpoint; d21 is a secondary endpoint. Frame the paper's claim as 'gradient improves d14 in vitro proliferation', not 'gradient improves bone formation'. Long-term BV/TV would require an in-vivo follow-up (out of scope for this plan).

protocol.rationale: The hypothesis is about proliferation, so the primary experiment is in non-osteogenic α-MEM + 10% FBS only — dexamethasone explicitly suppresses proliferation, β-glycerophosphate triggers spontaneous mineralisation that confounds DNA recovery. Daily medium change. n = 6 scaffolds per group per time point × 5 time points × 2 groups = 60 scaffolds; perfusion bioreactor with parallel chambers.

protocol.title: Serial alamarBlue (non-destructive) at d1, d4, d7, d14, d21

reagents.qty: 10% v/v in medium, 1 mL per scaffold

failure_warnings.mitigation: Run scaffold-only (no-cell) blanks per scaffold geometry every assay; subtract pixel/well-by-well. Read at 560/590 nm fluorescence with a low-laser-power / high-gain protocol. Verify signal-to-blank ratio > 3× before reporting.

protocol.rationale: alamarBlue is the orthogonal metabolic readout to PicoGreen. Non-destructive — same scaffolds can be re-read at every time point and then sacrificed for terminal endpoints. The (alamarBlue / PicoGreen) ratio across time is itself diagnostic: if it drops, cells are shifting from proliferative to differentiating state, which would invalidate proliferation-only conclusions.

protocol.title: Terminal sampling at each time point: lyse, PicoGreen DNA, DAPI cross-section

failure_warnings.mitigation: Use proteinase-K (200 μg/mL in TE, 56 °C × 30 min) + sonication (10 × 5 s pulses on ice) + 1% SDS chaotropic lysis — NOT single freeze-thaw + Triton, which recovers only 40–70% of dsDNA from 3D scaffolds. Validate recovery on 3 sentinel scaffolds (seed known cell number, immediately lyse, expect ≥ 80% recovery).

Run scaffold-only (no-cell) PicoGreen blank per scaffold geometry and per batch (every plate); subtract before quantification. Calibrate the PicoGreen standard curve with kit-supplied calf-thymus dsDNA; run a 'scaffold + known DNA spike' control to detect HA quenching of dye signal.

Sample DAPI cross-sections in three radial bins (outer, middle, inner) per scaffold, not just bulk; per-bin DNA differing > 2× outer→inner is a diffusion-limit signature even if total DNA is high (Step 8 mitigation cross-link).

protocol.rationale: PicoGreen DNA is the primary proliferation endpoint (specified by the hypothesis). DAPI confocal cross-sections are needed because the gradient hypothesis is intrinsically spatial — bulk PicoGreen alone cannot distinguish 'gradient redistributes cells' from 'gradient grows more total cells'. Live/dead at d14 only (one parallel scaffold per group) is a sanity check.

protocol.title: Secondary differentiation panel (parallel cohort, osteogenic medium): ALP, RUNX2/COL1/OCN qPCR

reagents.notes: Quantitative readout deferred until scaffold-only blank is validated; see failure mode below.

failure_warnings.mitigation: Daskalakis 2024: PCL/HA wins proliferation but PCL/Bioglass wins differentiation — the two markers can move in opposite directions across pore-architecture/composition. Report proliferation and differentiation as separate primary endpoints; do NOT attempt to combine them into a single 'osteogenicity score'.

ARS binds the scaffold itself (Battistelli 2025: standard curve is calibrated on synthetic HA). Always include scaffold-only no-cell blank; subtract. Cross-validate with von Kossa (silver phosphate stain) and EDX of cell-deposited mineral. Treat ARS as semi-quantitative on HA scaffolds.
β-glycerophosphate at 10 mM can produce cell-free mineral precipitates; run a no-cell scaffold + osteogenic medium parallel control to subtract baseline.

protocol.rationale: Secondary cohort runs in parallel with osteogenic medium (β-glycerophosphate + AsAP + dexamethasone) to track ALP activity (d7, d14), qPCR for RUNX2 / COL1A1 / OCN (d14), and ARS at d21 — answering the natural follow-up question 'does the proliferation effect translate to differentiation?'. Critically, this is a separate cohort because differentiation media confound proliferation readout; the primary hypothesis stands or falls on Step 10's proliferation data alone.

protocol.title: Statistics, data integrity, pre-registration

failure_warnings.mitigation: All representative images and quantification spreadsheets must be archived with original raw files. Do NOT splice or duplicate confocal panels — image-reuse is the most common retraction signature in this literature (8 retractions in raw/retractions/). Assign a second reviewer to spot-check images before any submission.
MC3T3 (cell line) is not donor-variable, but if extending to hBMSC follow-up: pool ≥3 donors, report inter-donor variance separately.

protocol.rationale: Pre-register the primary endpoint (d14 PicoGreen, one-sided two-sample t-test, α = 0.05, n = 6, expected effect size ≥ 25% per hypothesis) on OSF or equivalent before Step 7 begins. Report n, exact p-values, effect sizes, and Bayes factors. Power analysis target: detect 25% mean difference with σ = 15% (typical from Daskalakis 2024) at 80% power → n = 6 per group per time point.

materials.supplier: Various (Trizol, cDNA kit, qPCR mastermix, RUNX2/COL1A1/OCN/GAPDH primers)

materials.qty: Bundle (well plates, dishes, tubes, pipette tips, perfusion-chamber tubing)

materials.supplier: University CMC (e.g., Lithoz CeraFab 7500 / Tethon3D Bison)

materials.catalog: BET / XRD / ICP-MS / mercury intrusion / mechanical / SEM-EDX

budget.category: Scaffold raw materials (HA, photoinitiator, resin base)
budget.description: Plasma Biotal P88 HA + TPO + PEG-DA / diluents
α-MEM, FBS (single lot), P/S, L-glutamine, PBS, AsAP, β-GP, dexamethasone
PicoGreen P11496, alamarBlue, calcein-AM/EthD-1, DAPI, ARS, pNPP
Well plates, perfusion tubing, tips, tubes
BET, XRD, ICP-MS, mercury porosimetry, mechanical, SEM-EDX

timeline.name: design-and-slurry
deliverables: 60 vol% HA / TPO / PEG-DA slurry passing rheology spec

timeline.name: calibration
deliverables: S = d_post/d_CAD lookup table per pore bin

timeline.name: fabricate-and-characterise
deliverables: μCT, XRD, BET, ICP-MS, SEM, mechanical QC reports
Match-on-confound acceptance: porosity ±3 pp, BET ±10%, β-TCP <5%, Ca/P 1.667±0.05

timeline.phase: Phase 4 — Sterilise + ISO-10993 cyto pre-screen + cell expansion
timeline.name: release-and-cells
deliverables: STR-authenticated MC3T3 Subclone 4 stocks at passage ≤10

timeline.phase: Phase 5 — Seeding + perfusion culture + serial alamarBlue
timeline.name: culture
deliverables: d1, d4, d7, d14, d21 alamarBlue series

timeline.name: endpoint-and-stats
deliverables: PicoGreen DNA per scaffold per time point
ALP / qPCR / ARS for differentiation cohort

validation.primary_endpoint: Day-14 dsDNA content per scaffold (PicoGreen, lysate, blank-subtracted) — gradient vs uniform 300 μm control.
success_criteria: Gradient group d14 dsDNA ≥ 1.25× uniform-control d14 dsDNA (one-sided two-sample t-test, α = 0.05, n = 6 per group).

alamarBlue d14 fluorescence (blank-subtracted) directionally consistent with PicoGreen — ratio (alamarBlue / PicoGreen) within ±25% between groups, indicating both groups remained in proliferative phase.
Scaffold groups pass match-on-confound spec: total porosity within ±3 pp, BET within ±10%, XRD β-TCP fraction <5%, Ca/P 1.667 ± 0.05, mass within ±5%.
Seeding efficiency between groups within ±20%, no group-mean efficiency below 60% (perfusion).
L929 ISO-10993-5 viability ≥ 80% on scaffold-conditioned medium prior to cell-line release.
DAPI cross-sections show radial cell distribution consistent with the gradient design (gradient core cell density at least 50% of outer-zone density — confirms diffusion-mitigation claim).
failure_criteria: Gradient group d14 dsDNA < 1.25× uniform-control AND/OR p > 0.05: hypothesis falsified.
Match-on-confound spec violated (porosity > ±3 pp, BET > ±10%, β-TCP > 5%, Ca/P outside 1.617–1.717): result inadmissible — the gradient effect cannot be isolated from chemistry/porosity confound.

Seeding efficiency differs between groups > ±20% OR DAPI shows hollow large-pore core in gradient: cell-seeding-uniformity-loss-large-pores confound dominates — re-design seeding (longer perfusion, fibronectin coating).
Cytotoxic photoinitiator residue (L929 viability < 80% on conditioned medium): re-process scaffolds, do not seed MC3T3.
alamarBlue/PicoGreen ratio drifts > 50% by d14 — cells differentiating despite proliferation medium; revisit medium composition / cell passage.

Any imaging discrepancy or duplicated panel during second-reviewer image audit: full re-audit before any reporting (image-reuse-unsupported-osteogenesis-claims is the dominant retraction signature in this literature).

measurements.name: DAPI confocal cross-section radial cell density
qPCR RUNX2 / COL1A1 / OCN / GAPDH (osteogenic cohort)

failure_map.frequency_estimate: dominant — every AM route shows ≥10% deviation between CAD and as-built post-sintered pore size
failure_map.mitigation: Per-batch shrinkage calibration (Step 2) + μCT every experimental scaffold (Step 4) + reject scaffolds outside 100 ± 25 μm outer / 500 ± 50 μm core.

failure_map.frequency_estimate: dominant — 15–25% linear shrinkage of HA at 1200–1300 °C is the rule
failure_map.mitigation: Pre-compensate CAD by S = d_post/d_CAD ≈ 0.80; calibrate S per powder lot, slurry batch, sintering schedule; maximise HA solid loading (60–70 vol%).

failure_map.frequency_estimate: recurring — significant HA → β-TCP / α-TCP / CaO conversion above ~1300 °C
failure_map.mitigation: Cap sintering at 1200–1250 °C; verify Ca/P 1.667 ± 0.05 by ICP-MS pre and post; XRD per batch — reject if β-TCP fraction >5%.

failure_map.frequency_estimate: recurring for DLP HA — incomplete debinding leaves cytotoxic TPO/LAP residue
failure_map.mitigation: Slow 0.5 °C/min debinding ramp through 300–600 °C with ≥2 h soak + EtOH/water reflux leach + ISO-10993-5 L929 cytotoxicity gate (viability ≥80%).

failure_map.frequency_estimate: recurring — pores >400 μm lose 30–60% of statically-seeded cells
failure_map.mitigation: Dynamic perfusion seeding (4 h at 0.5 mL/min) + 10 μg/mL fibronectin pre-coating + per-scaffold seeding-efficiency QC; reject scaffolds with seeded count > 2 SD from group mean.

failure_map.frequency_estimate: dominant — affects nearly every static-cultured 3D scaffold >2 mm thick
failure_map.mitigation: Perfusion culture at ≥1× scaffold-volume turnover/min; CFD-predict permeability ≥10⁻⁹ m² before printing; DAPI cross-section radial bins to confirm core not necrotic.

failure_map.frequency_estimate: dominant — affects almost every fluorescence assay run on HA scaffolds without proper blanks
failure_map.mitigation: Scaffold-only no-cell blank per geometry per plate per time point; subtract; verify signal-to-blank > 3×; spectral imaging where possible.

failure_map.frequency_estimate: dominant — single freeze-thaw + Triton recovers only 40–70% of dsDNA from 3D scaffolds
failure_map.mitigation: Proteinase-K (200 μg/mL, 56 °C, 30 min) + sonication + 1% SDS chaotropic lysis; recovery validation experiment (known cell number, expect ≥80% recovery) before any group comparison.

failure_map.frequency_estimate: high — parental vs Subclone 4 vs Subclone 14 differ by ≥10× in mineralisation
failure_map.mitigation: Source MC3T3-E1 Subclone 4 from ATCC CRL-2593 (NOT parental, NOT Subclone 14, NOT the wiki's mis-labelled cellosaurus-mc3t3-e1-subclone4-cvcl5437.json file); STR-authenticate at receipt; passage ≤10 throughout.
Run primary experiment in non-osteogenic α-MEM + 10% FBS only (no β-GP, no AsAP, no dexamethasone); track differentiation in a parallel cohort (Step 11).
Scaffold-only no-cell blank per ARS plate; calibrate standard curve on synthetic HA; cross-validate with von Kossa + EDX of cell-deposited mineral.

failure_map.frequency_estimate: recurring retraction signature in this literature (8 retractions in raw/retractions/)
failure_map.mitigation: Archive all raw images with original filenames; second-reviewer image audit before submission; do not splice/duplicate panels.

scope_caveats: Karageorgiou & Kaplan 2005 (canonical '100 μm minimum, ~300 μm optimal') is non-OA and not in raw/ — claims invoking 'the canonical pore-size optimum' rely on transitive citation through Loh 2013 / Polo-Corrales 2014 / Baino 2015 / Bruzauskaite 2016. This plan avoids invoking that figure as primary justification.

The plan covers in-vitro proliferation only. In-vivo bone formation (BV/TV at 8–12 weeks) is the natural follow-up but out of scope here — failure-modes/in-vivo-vs-in-vitro-discordance and short-term-proliferation-vs-long-term-bone-formation flag the gap.

Native rabbit-femur Haversian/Volkmann canal diameters are 40–200 μm (Zhao 2026); the 500 μm core of this gradient is closer to trabecular spacing than cortical canal diameter. The biomimetic claim should be stated as 'cortical→cancellous trabecular-spacing mimicry' rather than 'cortical canal mimicry'.
under_supported_by_wiki: DLP slurry rheology / specific-gravity / cure-depth parameters for 60 vol% HA + TPO are not in raw/ — the wiki has the principles (slurry-preparation-for-DLP, DLP-vat-photopolymerization) but no protocol-level recipe; Step 2 calibration array is therefore broader than it would otherwise need to be. Pass-2 source for fix: a protocols.io HA-DLP recipe (not currently in raw/).

Quantitative shrinkage factor S for HA at 1250 °C is given as 0.75–0.85 in the wiki; Ishack 2015 used 1100 °C × 4 h on robocasted HA/β-TCP, which is a different system. Step 2 calibration is therefore essential, not optional.

ATCC and Sigma direct catalog pages were not captured in raw/ (failure-modes/ATCC-Sigma-vendor-pages-not-captured). The materials catalog numbers above are sourced from Cellosaurus / PubChem cross-references and standard published catalog conventions, not from live vendor snapshots — confirm at purchase.

Permeability CFD model parameters for HA scaffolds at 100→500 μm are not in any source page; Conoscenti 2025 used CFD for 70→200 μm PLLA. Step 8 mitigation references the principle but the lab will need to parameterise the model itself.
